{"database":"tpch_10","sql":"SELECT
  p.p_partkey,
  p.p_name,
  ROUND(SUM(l.l_extendedprice * (1 - l.l_discount)), 2) AS total_net_price,
  ROUND(SUM(l.l_extendedprice), 2) AS total_gross_extendedprice,
  SUM(l.l_quantity) AS total_quantity,
  COUNT(*) AS lineitems
FROM customer c
JOIN orders o   ON o.o_custkey = c.c_custkey
JOIN lineitem l ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey
JOIN part p     ON p.p_partkey = l.l_partkey
WHERE c.c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING'
GROUP BY 1,2
ORDER BY total_net_price DESC
LIMIT 20;"}

summary.text:
**Clarifying price calculation**

I'm thinking about how to present the results. "Total price" might refer to o_totalprice, but breaking it down requires clear allocation. It could be better to use net line revenue for clarity. I want to describe my assumptions and provide an SQL query for reproducibility. The user may want to see all parts instead of just the top results, but that could be overwhelming. So, I'll show just the top 20 and provide an option to remove the limit or show by part type. Let's respond!

content.text:
For the **BUILDING** market segment (`customer.c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING'`), here are the **top parts by total price** (computed as **net line revenue** = `SUM(l_extendedprice * (1 - l_discount))`).

### Top 20 parts (BUILDING segment) by total net price
| partkey | part name | total net price | total gross extendedprice | total qty | lineitems |
|---:|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 582955 | snow drab deep cyan almond | 1,140,119.94 | 1,200,340.77 | 589.00 | 17 |
| 795974 | chiffon plum thistle blanched floral | 1,131,180.81 | 1,190,215.50 | 575.00 | 15 |
| 1928878 | peach firebrick sandy peru snow | 1,079,542.56 | 1,142,161.22 | 599.00 | 19 |
| 986996 | firebrick salmon burlywood cornflower navajo | 1,077,551.69 | 1,131,041.85 | 543.00 | 18 |
| 580647 | navy ghost maroon rosy antique | 1,072,731.09 | 1,136,773.96 | 658.00 | 19 |
| 1923984 | rose violet white chartreuse ivory | 1,062,354.52 | 1,120,402.62 | 558.00 | 17 |
| 158995 | seashell honeydew peru steel coral | 1,029,090.07 | 1,080,398.74 | 526.00 | 15 |
| 395998 | dim lavender lace linen powder | 996,148.17 | 1,051,177.96 | 502.00 | 15 |
| 597964 | plum tomato violet wheat dodger | 995,607.73 | 1,045,403.58 | 507.00 | 13 |
| 724991 | orange mint papaya medium blanched | 988,868.70 | 1,040,235.36 | 516.00 | 16 |
| 1933917 | green lavender khaki aquamarine azure | 966,865.41 | 1,008,573.94 | 517.00 | 15 |
| 1181942 | papaya steel blue forest moccasin | 965,901.50 | 997,777.77 | 493.00 | 16 |
| 73933 | metallic frosted brown seashell blanched | 964,162.88 | 1,012,579.83 | 531.00 | 17 |
| 977997 | orchid hot burlywood deep cyan | 961,884.57 | 993,901.05 | 479.00 | 15 |
| 1655987 | chiffon powder royal turquoise azure | 960,025.75 | 1,018,079.60 | 524.00 | 15 |
| 1183941 | navy mint cornsilk floral peach | 947,972.50 | 986,121.43 | 487.00 | 17 |
| 1318902 | lime cornflower plum peach pink | 947,377.50 | 983,470.08 | 512.00 | 14 |
| 1593848 | spring peru misty magenta cream | 946,942.98 | 986,419.16 | 508.00 | 14 |
| 698832 | seashell indian peru steel drab | 942,202.91 | 983,139.60 | 537.00 | 15 |
| 705902 | sandy thistle snow red chartreuse | 941,018.72 | 1,005,447.49 | 527.00 | 13 |

SQL used (edit/remove `LIMIT` if you want all parts):
```sql
SELECT
  p.p_partkey,
  p.p_name,
  ROUND(SUM(l.l_extendedprice * (1 - l.l_discount)), 2) AS total_net_price,
  ROUND(SUM(l.l_extendedprice), 2) AS total_gross_extendedprice,
  SUM(l.l_quantity) AS total_quantity,
  COUNT(*) AS lineitems
FROM customer c
JOIN orders o   ON o.o_custkey = c.c_custkey
JOIN lineitem l ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey
JOIN part p     ON p.p_partkey = l.l_partkey
WHERE c.c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING'
GROUP BY 1,2
ORDER BY total_net_price DESC
LIMIT 20;
```

If by “total price” you meant **order header total** (`orders.o_totalprice`) rather than line revenue, say so and I’ll adjust (it’s not directly attributable to individual parts without an allocation rule).
